title: 'Those who are targeting Bengal should go to hell': Mamata launches fiery salvo at BJP
content: West Bengal Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee criticised the BJP during Eid-ul-Fitr celebrations in Kolkata, accusing the Centre of trying to remove voters through SIR and impose President’s Rule. She said her government would resist such moves and protect people’s rights. Her remarks come amid a political row over electoral roll scrutiny ahead of the assembly elections, with the TMC alleging attempts to target genuine voters and divide society.

title: IPL 2026: Rohit Sharma, Virat Kohli's fight for relevance before 2027 WC
content: Rohit Sharma and Virat Kohli face a crucial IPL season, their most visible audition for the 2027 ODI World Cup. With shrinking game-time and increasing selector pragmatism, this tournament is a fitness and form test.  They must prove their present-day relevance and endurance to secure a spot, as reputation alone won't suffice for the demanding ODI format.

title: Will Putin cut Iran loose for Ukraine? What Russian envoy said on quid pro quo claims
content: Russia has vehemently denied reports suggesting a quid pro quo offer to the US: halting intelligence sharing with Iran in exchange for reduced pressure on Ukraine. Russian officials, including envoy Kirill Dmitriev, labelled the claims as 'fake' and 'Western disinformation.' The alleged proposal, reportedly made to former Trump administration envoys, was also said to have been rejected by Washington.

title: Days after Amazon's complaint against Starlink, Blue Origin files for data centre satellites
content: Amazon's Blue Origin has applied to launch up to 51,600 orbiting data center satellites, citing insatiable AI demand.  The company argues these space-based servers will complement terrestrial infrastructure, offering a new compute tier independent of Earth's constraints. Blue Origin claims Project Sunrise will make AI compute more accessible and use spectrum efficiently, benefiting the public interest.

title: Raymond MD Gautam Singhania injured in Maldives speedboat crash; ace rally driver missing
content: Ace Indian rally driver Hari Singh, 59, is missing after a speedboat overturned in the Maldives. Gautam Singhania, chairman of Raymond Group, was among those rescued but sustained minor injuries. Search operations for Singh and another individual were ongoing.

title: ‘Dhurandhar 2’ day 2 box office: Spy thriller fails to cross Rs 230 crore mark
content: Ranveer Singh's 'Dhurandhar: The Revenge' shattered box office records with a Rs 226.27 crore net collection in India within its first two days. Despite a slight dip on day two, the film's paid preview shows and strong opening day performance highlight its massive impact.

title: Eid-ul-Fitr: Wishes, messages, quotes, greetings & images for sharing
content: Eid-ul-Fitr has arrived, marking the end of Ramadan with joyous celebrations.  This guide offers a collection of heartfelt, fun, and spiritual messages to share with loved ones via WhatsApp and social media.

title: Years after Zuckerberg called for Meta's 'next frontier’, company shuts down Horizon world
content: Meta is shutting down its virtual reality social network, Horizon Worlds, for Quest headsets by June 15, 2026. The platform, a significant metaverse investment, struggled to gain traction and will transition to a mobile-only experience. This move aims to streamline Meta's VR offerings and focus development efforts.

title: India's crude shuffle: How Hormuz chokehold rerouted oil imports from Gulf to Russia
content: India's crude oil imports have significantly dropped in March due to disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz, forcing refiners to pivot towards Russia and African producers. Russia has emerged as the largest supplier, nearly doubling its shipments, while African nations like Angola have also increased their exports to compensate for reduced Middle Eastern supplies.

title: Google announces it is shutting down Firebase Studio, less than a year after its launch
content: Google is shutting down its Firebase Studio AI workspace on March 22, 2027, less than a year after its April 2025 launch. The company is consolidating its AI developer offerings, moving lessons learned into Google AI Studio and Google Antigravity. Core Firebase services remain unaffected, with a year-long transition period for users.

title: Mohsin Naqvi-led PCB under scanner, Kirsten exposes ‘constant outside noise’
content: Former Pakistan head coach Gary Kirsten cited excessive interference from the Pakistan Cricket Board (PCB) as the primary reason for his abrupt departure after just six months. He described the constant external noise and punitive actions around poor performance as a significant challenge, making it difficult to implement plans or build relationships with players.

title: Is US‑Iran war ending soon? Trump considers 'winding down' military efforts
content: President Trump announced the US is nearing its military objectives in the Middle East, ruling out a ceasefire. He outlined five key goals, including degrading Iran's missile capabilities and preventing nuclear acquisition. Meanwhile, Iran's new Supreme Leader claimed victory over the US and Israel.Trump lists 5 strategic goals in Middle East war, Khamenei claims 'enemy has been defeated'

title: 'Syringe found in bathroom': Gurgaon nurse dies mysteriously; husband booked for dowry death
content: A woman died under suspicious circumstances at her home in Garhi Harsaru, with her family accusing her husband of administering a poisonous injection. Police have registered a dowry death case against the husband, Arun Kumar, and are investigating the incident after a syringe was recovered from the scene.

title: Tyre burst triggers bus fire in Andhra; Odisha MLA among passengers
content: A private bus en route from Bhubaneswar to Nabarangpur caught fire near Ramabhadrapuram in Andhra Pradesh on Saturday morning, reportedly due to a rear tyre burst. All 37 passengers, including Chitrakonda MLA Mangu Khillo, evacuated safely before the bus was completely gutted. Fire services personnel extinguished the blaze.

title: Ex-India star quits commentary, alleges 'colour discrimination' in BCCI setup
content: Former India leg-spinner Laxman Sivaramakrishnan has retired from the BCCI commentary panel. He cited a lack of opportunities for toss and presentation ceremonies over two decades. Sivaramakrishnan indicated this was a long-standing pattern. He also suggested colour discrimination might be a factor. His retirement highlights issues within TV production. Sivaramakrishnan was a prominent leg-spinner in the 1980s.

title: Fear returns as miscreants open fire at woodcutters in Imphal East village
content: Fresh tensions flared in Imphal East as armed miscreants, suspected to be from the Kuki community, fired upon Meitei villagers collecting firewood. No casualties were reported, but security was heightened. Meanwhile, heavy firing between Tangkhul Naga and Kuki groups continued in Ukhrul, with a high-level review meeting held by the Manipur CM.

title: Watch: Kim Jong Un’s daughter drives tank during military drill
content: Kim Jong Un's daughter, believed to be 13-year-old Kim Ju Ae, was seen driving a tank during military drills, fueling speculation she's being groomed as heir. This latest joint appearance follows numerous high-profile events, with state media highlighting their close bond.

title: US sends over 2500 marines to Middle East: Which 3 warships are carrying them?
content: The United States is significantly bolstering its military presence in the Middle East, deploying thousands of Marines and multiple warships amid escalating tensions with Iran. This strategic move aims to enhance operational readiness, offering options for evacuation, securing sea routes, and potential ground action.

title: Watch: US diplomatic facility near Baghdad airport in flames after attack
content: A fire erupted at a US diplomatic facility near Baghdad airport after a strike, with a pro-Iranian militant group claiming responsibility. This incident follows reports of air defence systems activating over the US embassy. The attack occurs amidst escalating regional tensions and retaliatory strikes between Iran-aligned groups and US interests in Iraq.

title: Fuel, food and finance: How US-Iran war might spill into your monthly household bills
content: Global conflicts, from the Middle East to Ukraine, are directly impacting Indian households. Expect higher grocery bills due to rising LPG, cooking oil, and pulse prices. Commuting costs are increasing with fuel surcharges on flights. Even education abroad and consumer electronics are becoming more expensive, while stock market losses affect savings. The ripple effects are undeniable.

title: After Russian, US eases sanctions on Iranian oil for one month
content: The US has granted a 30-day waiver on Iranian oil shipments currently at sea, aiming to stabilize global energy supplies amid escalating Middle East tensions. This move, intended to inject approximately 140 million barrels into the market, follows similar relaxations on Russian oil. While the US asserts control over revenue, Iran claims no surplus oil is available.

title: US taps emergency oil reserve, releases 45 mn barrels to tame war-driven price hike
content: Amidst the ongoing war with Iran, the Trump administration is releasing 45.2 million barrels from the Strategic Petroleum Reserve to curb soaring oil prices. This move, part of a global effort, involves lending crude to energy firms with repayment including a premium, aiming to stabilize markets without taxpayer cost. The reserve, holding significant oil, is expected to grow upon repayment.

title: 'Brotherhood and kindness': PM Modi extends greetings on Eid; celebrations begin across nation
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi and President Droupadi Murmu extended warm Eid-ul-Fitr greetings, emphasizing brotherhood and peace. Celebrations unfolded across the nation following the sighting of the Shawwal moon, marking the conclusion of Ramazan. Devotees gathered at Delhi's Jama Masjid for special prayers, with vibrant scenes of community spirit and festive greetings.

title: Iran fires 2 ballistic missiles towards US, UK military bases in Diego Garcia
content: Diego Garcia is a strategically located atoll in the central Indian Ocean that hosts a key US-UK military facility. Part of the British Indian Ocean Territory, the base is operated primarily by the United States and serves as a critical hub for air and naval operations across a vast region spanning Africa, the Middle East, and Asia.

title: Watch: Australian PM Albanese heckled at Eid prayers in Sydney over support for Israel
content: Tensions flared at an Eid prayer gathering in Western Sydney as a few attendees heckled Prime Minister Anthony Albanese and Home Affairs Minister Tony Burke, demanding their removal. Shouts of "genocide supporters" echoed, reflecting community anger over the government's Gaza response and Islamophobia.

title: 6,6,6,6,6! KKR's 21-year-old blasts brutal ton, rings warning bells
content: Young batter Angkrish Raghuvanshi's blistering 103* in a KKR intra-squad match has created a selection headache for the team. With a revamped squad featuring new overseas stars, head coach Abhishek Nayar faces the challenge of finalising combinations for their IPL 2026 campaign opener against Mumbai Indians on March 29.

title: Trump admin sues Harvard University over Jewish students’ civil rights
content: The Trump administration has sued Harvard University. The lawsuit alleges the university violated Jewish students' civil rights. This action is part of a broader campaign against the Ivy League school. The government claims Harvard ignored campus hostility and failed to enforce anti-harassment rules. The suit seeks to compel compliance with civil rights law and recover federal funds.

title: Dense fog engulfs Delhi-NCR; IMD forecasts more rain
content: Dense fog has enveloped Delhi and surrounding areas following overnight rainfall. Temperatures are expected to rise gradually after a significant drop, with the maximum hovering between 26-28 degrees Celsius. The Air Quality Index is predicted to remain in the 'moderate' category.

title: Iga Swiatek struggles raise concern amid Miami Open defeat
content: Iga Swiatek’s Miami Open loss has raised serious questions about her form and mental state. Experts like Rennae Stubbs and Caitlin Thompson believe her psychologist Daria Abramowicz’s role needs review. Swiatek herself admits tennis feels complicated right now. With no recent titles and visible struggles, her team may need changes to help her return to top form.

title: What’s going on between Luka Dončić and Anamaria Goltes? Custody battle explained
content: NBA star Luka Dončić and partner Anamaria Goltes are embroiled in a complex international custody battle for their two daughters, born in the US and Slovenia. The split, reportedly due to Dončić's limited time with his children during the NBA season, has led to legal filings in both California and Slovenia. Courts are now determining jurisdiction in this high-profile dispute.

title: NFL trade rumors: Kansas City Chiefs named ‘possible destination’ for Cameron Jordan
content: Following a disappointing 2025 season, the Kansas City Chiefs are reportedly targeting veteran defensive end Cameron Jordan. A cryptic social media post from Chris Jones fueled speculation, now confirmed by reports suggesting Jordan could be a "possible destination."  Adding the 36-year-old Pro Bowler would bolster the pass rush alongside George Karlaftis, aiming for a Super Bowl run.

title: Watch: Bravo under fire after ‘two chicks at home’ remark at KKR presser
content: At an IPL 2026 press event, Dwayne Bravo made a joking remark caught on live mic, creating an awkward moment. Ajinkya Rahane quickly intervened, warning about the recording. The clip went viral, amusing fans, while Kolkata Knight Riders removed the video to avoid controversy ahead of their match against Mumbai Indians.

title: UP man sees reference to ‘blue drum’ in wife’s phone, lets her go with lover
content: A Bulandshahr man, fearing for his life after finding cryptic social media posts on his wife's phone, agreed to a separation. Screenshots referencing "cement aur drum" and "neela drum" led him to believe his wife and her lover plotted his murder. Police intervention facilitated a mutual divorce, with the child remaining with the father.

title: Security ring, ID checks: Nervy Eid for Delhi's Uttam Nagar after youth lynching
content: Hastsal village in Delhi is under heavy security following a fatal Holi incident.  Police and paramilitary forces have restricted movement, creating an uneasy silence where festive preparations usually thrive. Despite the tension, residents emphasize that communal harmony remains strong, with locals working to counter rumors of migration and fearmongering.

title: 'I had lost around 7 kg': Shreyas Iyer recalls 'severe' experience before IPL 2026
content: Batter Shreyas Iyer overcame a severe spleen injury that caused major weight loss, working hard to regain fitness and return to cricket. Now leading Punjab Kings, he is focused on winning the IPL 2026 title. Emphasising teamwork and bonding, Iyer and Arshdeep Singh remain confident about delivering a strong season.

title: Trump says US 'close to meeting objectives' in Middle East, rules out ceasefire with Iran
content: President Trump stated the U.S. is nearing its military objectives in the Middle East concerning Iran, aiming to degrade its missile capabilities and defense industry. He firmly rejected a ceasefire, asserting the U.S. is "obliterating the other side" and has "won." Trump also indicated other nations should guard the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Reranking the 32 men's teams left in March Madness
content: And then there were just 32. How do these teams stack up against each other after surviving the first round?

title: Your guide to March Madness Day 2: Recaps, every winner's chance to advance, more
content: ESPN's reporters on-site across the country detail how each game was won Friday, plus look ahead to Sunday.

title: Florida starts title defense with 59-point pounding
content: Florida began its national title defense with the second-largest victory margin in NCAA tournament history, pounding Prairie View A&M 114-55 on Friday night.

title: Miami (OH)'s run ends in blowout loss to Vols
content: Ja'Kobi Gillespie hit six 3-pointers and scored 29 points as sixth-seeded Tennessee ended a fabulous season for Miami (Ohio) with a 78-56 win Friday in the first round of the NCAA tournament.

title: 'Drug transactions' allegedly on Holloway's phone
content: Prosecutors allege "drug transactions" were found on Crimson Tide basketball star Aden Holloway's phone after he was arrested Monday on a felony drug charge, per documents obtained by ESPN.

title: Clark grins and bears lost tooth in UCLA's win
content: UCLA's Skyy Clark shook off the pain after losing a chunk of his top front tooth late in Friday's first-round game against UCF and helped the Bruins secure a 75-71 victory.

title: Caldwell takes blame as Lady Vols drop 8th straight
content: Seventh-seeded NC State beat 10th-seeded Tennessee in the first round of the NCAA tournament Friday, handing the Lady Vols their eighth straight loss.

title: NCAA sues DraftKings for trademark infringement
content: The NCAA filed suit against DraftKings on Friday, accusing the sportsbook operator of trademark infringement for use of terms such as March Madness.

content: What were the coolest, weirdest and most notable things to happen in the men's NCAA tournament's first round? We've got some of the best right here.

title: Women's March Madness Final Four, champion predictions
content: ESPN talent cast their votes for which foursome will reach the Final Four. Will Phoenix be full of chalk?

title: Purdue's Smith leaps Hurley as NCAA assists king
content: Purdue's Braden Smith broke Bobby Hurley's NCAA all-time assists record Friday night, reaching 1,077 for his career during a first-round NCAA tournament game against Queens University.

title: LIU's 'fins up' chant has turned a near-empty gym into March magic
content: A few college basketball fanatics were looking for a local team to root for in 2023. They landed on the LIU Sharks.

title: Who's your first signing? Most annoying coach? Best city? ESPN's player survey﻿
content: LaLiga players answered ESPN's questions on everything from which one star they would sign for their team to those all-important EA FC player ratings.

title: 10 great signings, six great trades: The NFL free agency moves that Walder graded with an 'A'
content: We've graded more than 60 signings and trades over the past few weeks. But only 16 moves received top marks. We ranked those deals.

title: Yates' draft rankings: Top 50 prospects, plus scouting reports
content: Who are the best players in this year's class? Where do they stand out? Field Yates stacks his top 50 prospects.

title: Olney: The seven things I took away from MLB spring training
content: What did you miss at major league camps this spring while watching the World Baseball Classic? Here's what stood out to us from Florida and Arizona.

title: 'They're not even close': Why a Giannis trade might be even more complicated this summer
content: As the Giannis trade watch enters another offseason, a rare ownership structure and financial turmoil have left opposing teams wondering: Who's in charge here?

title: Men's tournament: How to bet the Duke, Michigan St. games and more Saturday
content: As you prepare for tipoff, here are our best bets for the second round, plus a pick for the new Eliminator game.

content: Odds for each team to advance through the bracket and to win the 2026 women's college basketball championship.

title: 2026 NCAA wrestling championships: Schedule, how to watch
content: It's time for the 2026 NCAA wrestling championships! This year crowned the first women's tournament champion. Check out how to watch now.

title: Transfer rumors, news: Mohamed Salah debating Saudi Pro League move
content: Mo Salah is still deciding his future, but a Saudi team is confident it can tempt Liverpool's sporting director to join. Transfer Talk has the latest.

title: Risers and fallers: Doncic stars, but is it time to drop Giannis?
content: Luka Doncic is on another planet, and Ayo Dosunmu and Daniss Jenkins are on the rise, but should fantasy basketball managers keep Giannis Antetokounmpo and Mikal Bridges rostered?

title: Potential busts to avoid in 2026
content: Who does Tristan H. Cockcroft think will underachieve in 2026? We have the names to steer clear of when drafting your rosters.

title: Bank holiday today: Are banks closed for Eid-ul-Fitr? State-wise list
content: the TOI Business Desk is a vigilant and dedicated team of journalists committed to delivering the latest and most relevant business news from around the world to readers of the Times of India . the desk ensures that TOI readers are well-informed about the ever-changing and dynamic world of commerce .

title: Assembly election LIVE: TMC releases first list of candidates ahead of Assam Assembly elections
content: In Puducherry, Union Minister Mansukh Mandaviya declared that the NDA would contest the upcoming Assembly polls under the leadership of current Chief Minister N. Rangasamy.

title: PM Modi, President Murmu extend Id-ul-Fitr greetings
content: President Murmu urged all to resolve to promote unity and harmony in society and contribute to the nation’s progress

title: Odissi dancer Madhumita Raut dies of cardiac arrest
content: Her works included compositions on poetry, choreographic compositions on poems of Goethe, and fusion dances with Balinese dancer Dia Tantri in the Netherlands

title: Air India deployed wrong aircraft for Vancouver; plane returned to Delhi
content: An apparent lapse in updating the list of requirements for the operation of flights to Canada resulted in the situation, where a wrong aircraft was deployed for the particular service, sources said

title: Arms, ammunition, 50 IEDs seized in Manipur
content: Security forces have been conducting search operations in Manipur since ethnic violence broke out two years ago

title: Air India, IndiGo, SpiceJet oppose government’s 60% free seat selection decision
content: The Federation of Indian Airlines (FIA), which represents the three airlines, has also urged the Civil Aviation Ministry to take steps to withdraw the decision.

title: Development without impact: Coastal residents of Kanniyakumari and Nagercoil constituencies voice discontent
content: Around 15 coastal villages fall under the jurisdiction of the Kanniyakumari and Nagercoil constituencies but residents of most of them say they are dissatisfied with the projects implemented

title: Beyond the Biennale: How murals are rewriting Kochi’s streets
content: As the Kochi Muziris Biennale spills beyond gallery walls, artists and collectives turn Fort Kochi and Mattancherry into a living canvas shaped by community, politics and time.

title: Man arrested for allegedly molesting Malayalam actor in Kochi
content: Police say the accused had been repeatedly sending messages to the actor on a social media platform, and she had blocked him

title: Residents demand desilting of Velachery lake as flooding, infra issues remain major concerns
content: Velachery, which became a separate constituency in 2008, lags behind on various fronts, say residents; they are worried by the routine flooding of the area, poor road conditions, and heavy traffic congestion caused largely by encroachments

title: I have been in favour of doves in West Asia or other conflicts, says Shashi Tharoor
content: Shashi Tharoor was asked to comment on the hawkish remarks emanation from both sides when it comes to the West Asia conflict which began three weeks ago, with no end in sight

title: From green to grey, in Goa
content: Goa’s controversial Section 39A of the Town and Country Planning (Amendment) Act, 2024, has triggered protests from residents. They claim that the law is opening large tracts of land that hold paddy fields, orchards, and forests to concretisation. This will impact the environment and local communities, resulting in water and electricity shortages and sewage treatment problems. Snehal Mutha examines how it will affect Goa’s ecosystems and its people

title: Kerala polls: Will G. Sudhakaran throw a spanner in the Left’s electoral prospects?
content: If the veteran communist leader’s grassroots connect gels with UDF’s electoral machinery, the contest in Ambalappuzha could be turned on its head. By framing his Independent candidature as an effort to uphold ‘communist values,’ he seeks to position himself within an ideological continuum, while tapping into ‘disillusionment among genuine cadres’

title: Kerala’s fisherfolk hope to be heard this election season
content: Their wish list includes government-backed price fixing mechanism to ensure fair returns, stringent enforcement of rules, optimum use of available Central assistance, better rehabilitation schemes and student scholarships

title: Maharashtra will be at the forefront of India’s orange economy: CM Fadnavis
content: The Maharashtra Chief Minister highlighted that the esports is emerging as “viable” career option for youngster and does not remain mere entertainment

title: Tripura Government accommodating fake private universities, Opposition alleges
content: Opposition members staged a walkout after bills concerning the setting up of three universities were adopted by majority votes in the State Assembly on March 20

title: West Bengal’s two-cornered contest is one between continuity and change
content: While the Trinamool Congress, which is battling an anti-incumbency of 15 years, is banking on cash incentive schemes and Bengali identity, the BJP wants to unseat Mamata Banerjee on grounds of alleged demographic change and corruption in the State

title: Cost concerns delay safer building codes despite Himalayan threats
content: India’s earthquake safety codes are under scrutiny after the government withdrew updated building standards backed by a decade of scientific research; the revision would have nearly doubled hazard estimates in the Himalaya and northeast, bringing them in line with global benchmarks

title: Kerala Assembly Elections 2026: Manjeswaram set for another photo finish as key players line up
content: The UDF has fielded sitting MLA A.K.M. Ashraf of the Indian Union Muslim League, the NDA is fielding former BJP State president K. Surendran once again and the LDF has fielded  Communist Party of India (Marxist) district secretariat member K.R. Jayanandan

title: Kerala Assembly Elections 2026: RSP’s battle for survival after a decade of legislative absence
content: Should the RSP fail to open its account again in the Assembly election, the resulting migration of cadre and leaders may well signal the end of the party as a significant force in Kerala politics

title: Fresh plea claims existence of Jain temple, gurukul at disputed Bhojshala-Kamal Maula mosque site
content: The court has granted two weeks time to the government authorities to file their objections after they raised questions at the maintainability of the plea as a public interest litigation (PIL). The matter has been listed for April 2

title: Himachal Pradesh’s growth in 2025-26 pegged at 8.3%, says economic survey
content: According to the survey, the per capita income of Himachal Pradesh is estimated at ₹283,626 in 2025–26, registering a 9.8% growth over the previous year and remaining ₹64,051 higher than the national average

title: Mirwaiz ‘barred’ from Friday prayers at Srinagar’s Jamia Masjid
content: Mirwaiz Umar Farooq also posted pictures purportedly showing a large contingent of security forces and police vehicles outside the gate of his residence in Nigeen locality of the city here.

title: Show cause notice illegal, arbitrary, say BJD MLAs who cross-voted in Rajya Sabha poll
content: Political parties can not issue any direction or whip to their members to vote in a particular manner or not to vote at the election, BJD MLAs respond to show-cause notices

title: Delhi HC restores plea on absence of Section 377-like provision in BNS
content: Section 377, after the Supreme Court decriminalised consensual same-sex relations, continued to apply to non-consensual acts, offences involving minors, and bestiality. The BNS replaced the IPC from July 1, 2024

title: Chhattisgarh Assembly passes Bill to curb malpractices in public examinations
content: The Bill prescribed imprisonment of up to 10 years and fines of up to ₹1 crore for specified violations

title: CAPF Bill says IPS officers’ deputation necessary for Union-State coordination, reserves 50% I-G rank posts for IPS
content: The Bill seeks to negate a May 23, 2025 Supreme Court judgment which asked the Home Ministry to “progressively reduce” IPS deputation in CAPFs up to the rank of I-Gs in the next two years

title: Communists fight elections to reflect rights of people, says Brinda Karat
content: She says parliamentary democracy under the Prime Minister Narendra Modi-led government at the Centre has been changed into ‘corporate democracy’

title: A true benevolent authoritarian enforces discipline for the greater good: Seeman
content: Naam Tamilar Katchi chief coordinator Seeman contends that without discipline, there will be no order; he bats for an economy centred on agriculture in T.N. and plans to create government-led industries based on local resources if his party emerges victorious in the Assembly election

title: BJP cadre in Ernakulam irked over Twenty20’s sway in seat allocation
content: Having contested 11 of the 14 Assembly constituencies in Ernakulam in 2021, the party is now likely to be restricted to just three, while the BDJS may have to settle for two, down from three seats last time

title: Congress high command will decide Cabinet reshuffle: Home Minister
content: G. Parameshwara says it is natural to have aspirations, and there is nothing wrong in seeking a ministerial post.

title: Bypolls: BJP ahead in candidate selection, Congress weighs options
content: The bypolls have been necessitated owing to the deaths of incumbent MLAs H.Y. Meti in Bagalkot and Shamanur Shivashankarappa in Davangere; both served as Ministers during Siddaramaiah’s previous tenure as Chief Minister

title: UNI office sealed in Delhi; staffers allege manhandling by the police
content: An order issued by the Land and Development Office, pursuant to a court judgment, had asked the United News of India agency to vacate the premises

title: SC says people have a right to criticise its judgments
content: The Chief Justice said that the textbook edition in question had also balanced criticism by highlighting meaningful interventions made by the apex court in the past

title: Lok Ayukta orders Kerala govt. to rehabilitate families displaced for Veli tourist village
content: The panel directs the State government to file an Action Taken Report on September 3, when it again considers the case.

title: Assembly elections 2026 updates: Mamata releases TMC manifesto, claims 'unofficial President's Rule' imposed in poll-bound Bengal
content: Himanta Biswa Sarma files nomination from Assam's Jalukbari constituency; Mr. Sarma laid down his objective for Assam, centred around the identity and development of Assam

title: Himachal govt. intends to invest ₹3,000 crore in tourism; Chief Minister woos industry
content: During CII conference, Mr. Sukhu assured that harassment of industrialists would not be tolerated and their grievances could be brought directly to his notice; he adds that all clearances for industries should be provided at one place and the Industries department should actively facilitate investors

title: Every person with a phone today is media, a threat to free trial: SC
content: a bench of cJI Surya Kant, justices Joymalya Bagchi and Vipul M Pancholi alleged that police upload videos and photographs of arrested accused on their social media handles . the bench agreed with senior advocate Gopal Sankaranarayanan that "every person with a mobile has become media" the bench said that when there is an accident, people take out their mobile phones to create content even when a person is bleeding on the road .

title: "Call Got Disconnected": Family Claims UP Man Killed In Riyadh Missile Attack
content: Ravi Gopal from Mahmudabad had been working as a driver in a plastic factory in the Saudi capital since September 2025, his family said.

content: The US and Israel have targeted infrastructure for satellites and rockets, part of an effort to destroy technology that could be used for weapons programs, an Iran expert said.

title: War Expands To New Theatre? Iran Fires Missiles At Indian Ocean US-UK Base

title: Israel-Iran War LIVE: Video Shows Huge Fire At US Base In Baghdad After Drone Attacks
content: US Israel Iran War News LIVE Updates: Iran has been striking its Persian Gulf neighbours' energy facilities since the war started on February 28, and has made the Strait of Hormuz shipping channel, nearly impassable.

title: Delhi-NCR Sees Rare Fog After Recording Coldest March Day In 6 Years
content: As of 7.20 am, the temperature recorded at Delhi's Safdrajung observatory was 14 degrees Celsius, while the humidity stood at 100 per cent.

title: Iran Hangs 3, Including Teen Accused Of Killing Cops During January Protests
content: Iran executed three men on Thursday who were accused of killing police officers during protests in January.

title: Trump Says He Doesn't Want Ceasefire With Iran
content: US President Donald Trump on Friday ruled out reaching a ceasefire agreement with Iran, saying Washington has the upper hand in the three-week-old war.

title: "Getting Close To Our Objectives": Trump Considering 'Winding Down' Iran War
content: President Donald Trump said on Friday the US was getting very close to meeting its objectives as it considers winding down its military efforts in the Iran war.

title: Elon Musk Misled Investors During 2022 Twitter Purchase, Finds Jury
content: A jury has found Elon Musk liable for misleading investors by deliberately driving down Twitter's stock price in the tumultuous months leading up to his 2022 acquisition.

title: US Pauses Sanctions On Iranian Oil At Sea Amid Soaring Prices
content: The authorization allows for the delivery and sale of Iranian crude oil and other petroleum products loaded onto ships before March 20, and will last through April 19, the US said.

title: Why DC coach expects Defenders to get every team's best shot
content: Shannon Harris was named interim head coach of the DC Defenders . "stay away from the rat poison," Harris says . he says the attention to detail in the preseason has been what he's wanted . the defenses have a lot of new guys on our team, but the culture is set," Harris said .

title: Avalanche first NHL team to clinch playoff berth
content: the Colorado Avalanche became the first NHL team to clinch a playoff spot . the franchise had dropped four of five, including a 7-2 home loss to a Pittsburgh team that was playing without Sidney Crosby . but coach Jared Bednar was encouraged by how the team played in a 2-1 shootout loss .

title: Sources: 49ers pass on Williams option, eye deal
content: the 49ers ' are not exercising the $10 million option bonus on Trent Williams' contract . the team is not overly concerned about it, sources tell ESPN's Adam Schefter . Williams' salary cap number now will grow to close to $47 million, sources say .

title: VAR review: Should Man United have gotten a penalty? Why was Maguire sent off?
content: we take a look at the major incidents to examine and explain the process in terms of VAR protocol and the Laws of the Game . Andy Davies (@andydaviesref) is a former Select Group referee, with over 12 seasons on the elite list, working across the Premier League and Championship .

title: Power Rankings: How did Arsenal drop and Man City rise in ratings of all teams?
content: the four teams in the relegation battle combined to win four total points . aston villa and liverpool combined for three points in the last two weeks . arsenal have drawn in back-to-back matches against forest and west Ham .

title: 'The best is still yet to come': Why Wyatt Langford is an AL MVP sleeper pick
content: outfielder Wyatt Langford landed at no. 4 on the top 100 prospects list of ESPN's Kiley McDaniel . he had spent just 44 games in the minor leagues at that point, yet broke camp with the defending champions as their every-day left fielder . his rapid development meant heightened external expectations .

title: How Chuck Norris Facts gave birth to the modern meme
content: the sports world was heartbroken to find out that Chuck Norris died on friday . this story was originally published on august 23, 2023 . it was the internet equivalent of Marie Curie discovering radium, or Thomas Edison inventing the telegraph.
